title: You have to work for litigants: Delhi High Court questions advocates' decision to strike
description: The Delhi High Court on Monday questioned the Delhi High Court Bar Association (DHCBA)‘s decision to go on strike, stating that such a move only affects the litigants. The DHCBA is abstaining from work today against a proposal to increase the pecuniary jurisdiction of district courts from ₹2 crore to ₹20 crore. When his court assembled, Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav questioned a proxy counsel appearing before him as to what the strike was about. The counsel told the Bench
